task: Write a Python function sum_distinct(nums) returning the sum of the distinct values in the list, each value counted once no matter how many times it repeats. An empty list returns 0. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code accurately handles the task of summing distinct values in a list, while being both concise and readable.
trace: I used Python's built-in `set()` data type to remove any duplicate values from the input list, then converted it back into a regular list and applied the `sum()` function. This approach ensures that each value is counted only once. To handle an empty list, I set the default return value to 0.
